 {"id":"gt-1u9","title":"Interactive prompts for destructive operations","description":"Add interactive confirmations for destructive operations.

## Operations Needing Confirmation
- `gt swarm cancel` - Cancels active swarm
- `gt swarm land --force` - Force landing
- `gt polecat decommission` - Removes polecat
- `gt rig remove` - Removes rig
- `gt stop --all` - Stops all sessions
- `gt mail purge` - Permanently deletes mail

## Implementation Options

### Option 1: promptui library
```go
import "github.com/manifoldco/promptui"

prompt := promptui.Prompt{
    Label:     "Delete polecat Toast",
    IsConfirm: true,
}
result, err := prompt.Run()
```

### Option 2: Simple stdin
```go
func confirm(prompt string) bool {
    fmt.Printf("%s [y/N]: ", prompt)
    var response string
    fmt.Scanln(&response)
    return strings.ToLower(response) == "y"
}
```

## Bypass Flags
All confirmations skippable with --force or --yes:
```go
if !force && !confirm("Really cancel swarm?") {
    return nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Destructive ops prompt by default
- [ ] --force or --yes bypasses
- [ ] Clear prompt text explaining action
- [ ] Non-interactive mode (piped input) auto-fails","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:51.551594-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:54.283696-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-1uhmj","title":"Agent-centric dashboard view (gt dashboard)","description":"Charmbracelet TUI for gt feed with dashboard + stream layout.

## Layout (horizontal split)

**Top panel: Agent Tree (role/town view)**
- Shows EVERY agent organized by role
- Each agent shows their MOST RECENT activity inline
- At a glance: who is doing what RIGHT NOW
- Collapsible sections

**Bottom panel: Event Stream (chronological feed)**
- Full scrollable history of events
- Most recent at top
- More detail than the tree view
- This is what you scroll through

## Visual
```
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| GT Feed                                    Filter: all      |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| gastown/                           <- AGENT TREE (top)      |
|   mayor      [10:30] Dispatched gt-xyz -> Toast             |
|   witness    [10:28] Nudged polecat-3 (idle 5m)             |
|   refinery   [10:25] Merged PR #123                         |
|   crew/                                                      |
|      joe        [10:22] Completed gt-95j13                  |
|      max        [10:18] Working on gt-s6r44                 |
|   polecats/                                                  |
|      Toast      [10:30] Received gt-xyz                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| [10:30] Toast: Received molecule gt-xyz                     |
| [10:28] witness: Nudged polecat-3 after 5m idle             |
| [10:25] refinery: Merged PR #123                            |
| ...                                        <- EVENT STREAM  |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| j/k: scroll  /: search  f: filter  q: quit  ?: help         |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Why this layout?
- Top answers: what is everyone doing?
- Bottom answers: what just happened?
- Together: complete situational awareness

## Replaces
- gt feed (currently just wraps bd activity)
- gt dashboard task merges into this

## Tech
- bubbletea Model with two viewports
- Tab switches focus between tree/stream
- j/k scrolls focused panel","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T12:46:38.812289-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-28T16:23:51.095679-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T16:23:51.095679-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-1uhmj","depends_on_id":"gt-38doh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T12:46:47.3704-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-1uhmj","depends_on_id":"gt-lexye","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:14:56.174801-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-1y0e","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: Investigate beads-sync divergence (221 files)","description":"## Context

gt doctor now detects orphaned code on beads-sync branch. Running it shows:

  beads-sync-orphans: 221 file(s) on beads-sync not in main

This is in the gastown repo (checking from crew/max).

## Background

- Earlier today we recovered orphaned mail migration code lost in merge 96c773f
- Recently simplified architecture: all workers use mayor/rig/.beads (redirects)
- This simplification may have caused beads-sync branch to diverge unexpectedly

## Investigation Steps

1. From crew/max run: git diff main..beads-sync --stat
2. Check if beads-sync should even exist anymore (was for multi-clone sync)
3. If obsolete with redirect architecture, consider deleting it
4. If it has legitimate changes, cherry-pick or merge to main

## Key Question

With all workers using mayor/rig/.beads via redirects, is beads-sync still needed?

## Commands

git log main..beads-sync --oneline | head -20
git diff main..beads-sync -- *.go | head -100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00"}

Part of epic gt-u7dxq","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/crew/jack","created_at":"2025-12-28T11:02:14.269602-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:05.875081-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:05.875081-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in beads: added Actor field to MutationEvent, fixed status mutations to use new assignee, updated activity display to show actor"}

 {"id":"gt-1z4m","title":"gt swarm is implemented but undocumented","description":"internal/swarm/ package is fully implemented with:

 {"id":"gt-26pib","title":"Day 3.6: Polecat closes steps for activity feed","description":"**REVISED**: Step completion is for visibility, not session control.

Polecats close molecule steps as they complete them:
```bash
# After completing implement step:
bd close <step-bead-id>  # Or: mol step done implement
```

This provides:
1. **Activity feed visibility** - Observers see progress in real-time
2. **Audit trail** - Step completion timestamps in beads
3. **Stuck detection** - Witness can see 'long time on same step'

What this does NOT do:
- Trigger session recycle (polecat self-manages)
- Block next step (polecat continues immediately)
- Require Witness intervention

Implementation options:
- Option A: Polecat runs `bd close <step-id>` manually
- Option B: Formula `on_complete` hook auto-closes step bead
- Option C: `mol step done <step>` convenience command

Priority lowered: Nice-to-have for visibility, not launch-blocking.

**Dependency removed**: No longer depends on gt-zb0io (recycle between steps).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:02:12.671367-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T22:50:26.895789-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-26pib","depends_on_id":"gt-zb0io","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:02:45.359036-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-2g130","title":"Remove dead code: splitLines first loop, unused methods","description":"Remove dead code: splitLines first loop, unused methods.

## Dead code locations

### 1. internal/cmd/polecat.go (lines 1093-1106)
splitLines function has unreachable first loop:
```go
func splitLines(s string) []string {
    var lines []string
    for _, line := range filepath.SplitList(s) {  // DEAD: wrong function
        ...
    }
    lines = nil  // Discards above
    for _, line := range strings.Split(s, "\n") {  // This is what runs
        lines = append(lines, line)
    }
    return lines
}
```
**Fix:** Remove first loop and reset.

### 2. internal/townlog/logger.go (lines 277-279)
```go
func (e Event) JSON() ([]byte, error) {
    return json.Marshal(e)
}
```
**Fix:** Remove unused method.

### 3. internal/lock/lock.go (line 27)
```go
ErrStaleLock = errors.New("stale lock detected")
```
**Fix:** Remove unused error variable.

### 4. internal/witness/types.go (line 19)
```go
StatePaused State = "paused"
```
**Fix:** Remove unused state constant.

### 5. internal/refinery/manager.go (lines 527-538)
```go
func (m *Manager) getTestCommand() string { ... }
```
**Fix:** Remove unused method.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] polecat.go: Dead loop removed from splitLines
- [ ] townlog/logger.go: Event.JSON() method removed
- [ ] lock/lock.go: ErrStaleLock removed
- [ ] witness/types.go: StatePaused removed
- [ ] refinery/manager.go: getTestCommand removed
- [ ] go build ./... passes
- [ ] go test ./... passes","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:43:17.034253-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-28T17:13:10.76452-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T17:13:10.76452-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-2kz","title":"CLI: cleanup commands for stale state","description":"Cleanup commands for recovering from stale state.

## Commands

### gt cleanup <polecat>
Clean stale state for specific polecat.
```
gt cleanup <rig>/<polecat> [--dry-run]
```

Actions:
- Remove orphaned state.json if clone missing
- Clear stale session references
- Reset stuck state (working → idle after timeout)

### gt cleanup --all
Clean all polecats in workspace.
```
gt cleanup --all [--dry-run]
```

## Implementation
```go
func CleanupPolecat(rigName, polecatName string, dryRun bool) (*CleanupResult, error)

type CleanupResult struct {
    OrphanedStateRemoved bool
    SessionsCleared      int
    StateReset           bool
    Warnings             []string
}
```

## Overlap with Doctor
- Doctor diagnoses and offers --fix
- Cleanup is more aggressive state recovery
- Consider merging into doctor --fix

## New File
internal/cmd/cleanup.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Removes orphaned state files
- [ ] Clears stale session refs
- [ ] --dry-run shows what would happen
- [ ] Reports all actions taken","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:31.944982-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:12.430696-08:00"}
